task: Add a preliminary step that ensures the src/middleware directory exists (for example by running fs.mkdirSync('src/middleware', { recursive: true }) in your setup or build script), then create src/middleware/fortress.js exporting CommonJS modules: (1) an Express middleware function that sets common security headers and, if process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS==='true', adds Strict-Transport-Security; (2) an async Koa middleware (ctx, next) that does the same; and (3) an Express Router named router with GET /status/fo returning 200 JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true }, without touching any other files yet.

subtasks.description: Create src/middleware/fortress.js exporting CommonJS modules: an Express middleware setting common security headers (conditionally adding HSTS if process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS==='true'), a Koa async middleware doing the same, and an Express Router named router with GET /status/fo returning { status: 'ok', fortress: true }.
subtasks.test_command: node -e "const m=require('./src/middleware/fortress.js'); if(typeof m.expressFortress!=='function') throw new Error('expressFortress missing'); if(typeof m.koaFortress!=='function') throw new Error('koaFortress missing'); if(!m.router || typeof m.router.get!=='function') throw new Error('router missing or invalid'); console.log('ok');"
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: src/middleware/fortress.js exists and uses CommonJS exports. Exports: expressFortress(req,res,next) that sets common security headers and, if process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS==='true', adds Strict-Transport-Security; koaFortress(ctx,next) that sets the same headers; router is an Express Router with a GET /status/fo route returning HTTP 200 and JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true }.

plan_summary: Ensure the src/middleware directory exists via a setup command, then add a new CommonJS module at src/middleware/fortress.js exporting: (1) an Express security middleware, (2) a Koa security middleware, and (3) an Express Router with GET /status/fo endpoint.
risk_assessment: Low — Main risks are missing Node/Express installation in the environment or shell quoting differences. Global setup commands mitigate directory creation and dependency issues.
